output.summary: Fix doubled 32px spacing when header becomes fixed on scroll. The mt-8 margin on .header-main creates 32px in normal flow, but when fixed, (a) the margin pushes the header down from its top value (adding to --fixed-offset which already includes +32), and (b) the spacer div uses offsetHeight which excludes margin, so it under-compensates by 32px.

rootCause.description: Three interacting issues cause the doubled spacing when fixedOnScroll is enabled:

issues.title: mt-8 margin persists during fixed positioning
issues.detail: When .header-main gets position:fixed via the .scrolling class, the mt-8 (32px margin-top) still applies. This pushes the header 32px below its `top` value. Since --fixed-offset already includes a hardcoded +32 (HeaderMain.js line 151), the header ends up 64px from the top instead of 32px.

issues.title: Spacer height excludes margin
issues.detail: showSpacer(header.offsetHeight) on JS line 173 uses offsetHeight, which includes content+padding+border but NOT margin. In normal document flow, the header occupied offsetHeight + 32px margin-top. The spacer is therefore 32px shorter than the space the header actually occupied, causing a 32px upward content jump.

issues.title: --fixed-offset has hardcoded +32 that duplicates mt-8
issues.detail: calculateFixedOffset() on JS line 151 adds a hardcoded 32px to the offset. This was originally meant to create the visual gap that mt-8 provides. But since mt-8 is still on the element when fixed, the gap is doubled.

proposedFix.strategy: Remove mt-8 from .header-main when it becomes fixed (via CSS), and include the margin in the spacer height calculation. The --fixed-offset +32 stays as-is because it correctly positions the fixed header with the desired gap from the top. The mt-8 just needs to be neutralized when fixed.
proposedFix.approach: Use a CSS rule to set margin-top:0 on .header-main when it has the .scrolling class (i.e., when fixed). Include the margin in the spacer height so the space below is correctly preserved.

changes.description: Add [&.scrolling]:mt-0 to the .header-main element's class list. This removes the margin-top when the header becomes fixed, preventing the doubled gap. The --fixed-offset +32 already provides the correct top spacing.
changes.detail: On line 59, in the class string that starts with 'header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full mt-8 transition-[transform,opacity]', add [&.scrolling]:mt-0 after mt-8. Result: 'header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full mt-8 [&.scrolling]:mt-0 transition-[transform,opacity]'

changes.description: Same change in the TSX editor component. Add [&.scrolling]:mt-0 to the .header-main element's class list.
changes.detail: On line 498, in the cn() object, the first key string 'header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full mt-8 transition-[transform,opacity]' should become 'header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full mt-8 [&.scrolling]:mt-0 transition-[transform,opacity]'

changes.description: Include the margin-top in the spacer height calculation. Change showSpacer(header.offsetHeight) to include the computed margin-top of the header element.
changes.detail: On line 173 (and line 300 in openMenu), change showSpacer(header.offsetHeight) to include the margin. Before calling showSpacer, compute the margin: const headerMarginTop = parseInt(window.getComputedStyle(header).marginTop, 10) || 0; then call showSpacer(header.offsetHeight + headerMarginTop). This must be done BEFORE the .scrolling class is added (which zeroes the margin via FIX-1), so the margin is captured while still in normal flow. The current code on line 173 already runs before line 176 (header.classList.add('scrolling')), so the order is correct.

fixedDesktop_normalMobile.behavior: On desktop scroll: .scrolling added -> mt-0 applied via [&.scrolling]:mt-0 -> header fixed at top with --fixed-offset gap -> spacer includes margin height. On mobile: no .scrolling class ever added, mt-8 stays, no spacer shown. Correct.

fixedDesktop_fixedMobile.behavior: Both breakpoints: .scrolling added on scroll -> mt-0 via [&.scrolling]:mt-0 -> spacer includes margin. Correct.

normalDesktop_fixedMobile.behavior: On mobile scroll: .scrolling added -> mt-0 applied -> spacer includes margin. On desktop: no .scrolling, mt-8 stays. Correct.

noFixed.behavior: No .scrolling class ever added. mt-8 always present. No spacer shown. No change from current behavior. Correct.

overlay_fixed.behavior: Spacer is already skipped when displayAboveContent is true (JS line 173 condition: !displayAboveContent). The header uses absolute positioning initially, then transitions to fixed on scroll. [&.scrolling]:mt-0 still applies correctly to remove the margin gap when fixed. The -mb-[var(--header-main-height)] on the outer wrapper handles the overlay offset. Correct.

overlay_noFixed.behavior: Header is absolute-positioned. No .scrolling class. mt-8 stays (creates gap between SEP button and header bar, or from top of wrapper). No spacer. No change. Correct.

decisions.decision: Use [&.scrolling]:mt-0 to remove margin when header is fixed, rather than removing mt-8 entirely and using padding/gap on a parent
decisions.evidence: The codebase already uses [&.scrolling] selector patterns extensively (e.g., [&.scrolling]:fixed, [&.scrolling]:top-(--fixed-offset)). Adding [&.scrolling]:mt-0 follows the same pattern and is minimal-impact.

decisions.decision: Capture marginTop from computed style before .scrolling is added, include it in spacer height
decisions.evidence: The spacer must compensate for the full space the header occupied in normal flow. offsetHeight excludes margin. Reading marginTop before adding .scrolling class (which zeroes it) gives the correct value.

The +32 in --fixed-offset represents the desired visual gap from the viewport top when fixed. With mt-8 removed via [&.scrolling]:mt-0, the +32 is the sole source of this gap, which is correct behavior.

testingNotes: Test with fixedOnScrollDesktop=true, scroll down past the header — header should sit at --fixed-offset from viewport top with no extra gap
Test with fixedOnScrollMobile=true on mobile viewport — same behavior
Test that when scrolling back to top (header returns to normal flow), the mt-8 gap reappears
Test displayAboveContent=true + fixedOnScroll — no spacer, header transitions smoothly from absolute to fixed
Test without fixedOnScroll — mt-8 always visible, no changes from current behavior
Verify no layout shift (CLS) when header transitions between fixed and normal
